title: The City of Eleven Gates
summary: The soul residing within the physical body and its independence from the body. Addresses health anxiety, sickness, and separating identity from the physical body.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “There is the city of eleven gates belonging to Him who is unborn and of unchanging consciousness. By meditating upon Him, one grieves no more; and being liberated one becomes free from…” and develops toward “" Neither the sun, the moon , the stars, nor the lightning shines here, nor to speak of this fire. Every other thing shines following That and due to…”

verses.sanskrit: पुरमेकादशद्वारमजस्यावक्रचेतसः । अनुष्ठाय न शोचति विमुक्तश्च विमुच्यते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.translation: There is the city of eleven gates belonging to Him who is unborn and of unchanging consciousness. By meditating upon Him, one grieves no more; and being liberated one becomes free from birth and death. This is indeed That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit: हँसः शुचिषद्वसुरन्तरिक्षसद्- होता वेदिषदतिथिर्दुरोणसत् । नृषद्वरसदृतसद्व्योमसद् अब्जा गोजा ऋतजा अद्रिजा ऋतं बृहत् ॥
verses.translation: The Atman is the Sun in the heavens, the air in the sky, the fire in the altar, the guest in the house; He dwells in men, in Gods, in the Sacrifice, in space. He is all that is born in water, all that is born on earth, in the sacrifice and on the moutains. He is the True and the Great.

verses.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं प्राणमुन्नयत्यपानं प्रत्यगस्यति । मध्ये वामनमासीनं विश्वे देवा उपासते ॥
verses.translation: He it is that leads the life-breath upwards and casts the lower breath downwards. All the senses minister to Him, the Adorable, who is seated in the centre.

verses.sanskrit: अस्य विस्रंसमानस्य शरीरस्थस्य देहिनः । देहाद्विमुच्यमानस्य किमत्र परिशिष्यते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.translation: When the soul in the body is separated and leaves the body what is left over here? This is indeed That. (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit: न प्राणेन नापानेन मर्त्यो जीवति कश्चन । इतरेण तु जीवन्ति यस्मिन्नेतावुपाश्रितौ ॥
verses.translation: Man does not live by breath alone, but by Him who is the power of breath.

verses.sanskrit: हन्त त इदं प्रवक्ष्यामि गुह्यं ब्रह्म सनातनम् । यथा च मरणं प्राप्य आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥
verses.translation: Listen, I shall tell you O ! Gautama, of the secret, eternal Brahman, and what happens to the self after meeting death.

verses.sanskrit: योनिमन्ये प्रपद्यन्ते शरीरत्वाय देहिनः । स्थाणुमन्येऽनुसंयन्ति यथाकर्म यथाश्रुतम् ॥
verses.translation: Some souls seek a womb for the sake of a physical body and yet others go to immovables, like the plant kingdom, depending upon the activities in the past and also in consonance with the level of awakening they have reached.

verses.sanskrit: य एष सुप्तेषु जागर्ति कामं कामं पुरुषो निर्मिमाणः । तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते । तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.translation: This principle Purusha keeping awake when others sleep, creating objects of desires , that which is taintless and said to be deathless is the Brahman. That is the substratum of all the worlds, and no one can surpass it . This , indeed is that. (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit: अग्निर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव । एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
verses.translation: As fire, though one only , having entered the world, takes the form of various objects (which it burns ) so also though the all permeating brahman is one only, takes the form apparently of the various objects it enters and is also beyond them.

verses.sanskrit: वायुर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव । एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
verses.translation: As one air, takes the shape of every object it enters, so the Self which permeates into everything , though one, takes the shape of every object in which it dwells. It also exists outside them.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्यो यथा सर्वलोकस्य चक्षुः न लिप्यते चाक्षुषैर्बाह्यदोषैः । एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा न लिप्यते लोकदुःखेन बाह्यः ॥
verses.translation: Like the sun, which is the eye of the whole world is not tainted by the outward defects of the objects it lights, which are seen by the eye, in the same manner, this Brahman which is inherent in all beings is not tainted by the sufferings of the world, as it is beyond the world.

verses.sanskrit: एको वशी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा एकं रूपं बहुधा यः करोति । तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीराः तेषां सुखं शाश्वतं नेतरेषाम् ॥
verses.translation: Eternal bliss falls to the lot of those wise ones who realise Him, existing within, the one controller, the inner being of all beings and being are manifests in myriad forms and not to the lot of others.

verses.sanskrit: नित्योऽनित्यानां चेतनश्चेतनानाम् एको बहूनां यो विदधाति कामान् । तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीराः तेषां शान्तिः शाश्वती नेतरेषाम् ॥
verses.translation: Those wise persons who realise Him, who is within, who is permanent among all others which are impermanent , who is sentient amongst which are insentinent and though one fulfills the desires of the several, they have lasting (inner) peace and none others.

verses.sanskrit: तदेतदिति मन्यन्तेऽनिर्देश्यं परमं सुखम् । कथं नु तद्विजानीयां किमु भाति विभाति वा ॥
verses.translation: How shall I know that supreme, unspeakable Bliss which they realize directly as 'This'? Is it self-effulgent - does It shine dinstinctly, or does It not?

verses.sanskrit: न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः । तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ॥
verses.translation: " Neither the sun, the moon , the stars, nor the lightning shines here, nor to speak of this fire. Every other thing shines following That and due to its effulgence everything is illumined.
